mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators looking to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the necessary components.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Cluster API Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components into the specified namespace, which is required for managing clusters.

```bash

kubectl create namespace capi-system

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.3

```

> Creates a namespace for Cluster API components.
> Installs Cluster API with the specified version.
commands: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.3

steps.title: Install the Cluster API Provider
steps.description:
This step installs a specific provider for Cluster API, which allows it to manage clusters on a particular infrastructure.

```bash

helm install cluster-api-provider-aws cluster-api/cluster-api-provider-aws --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.3

```

> Installs the AWS provider for Cluster API.
commands: helm install cluster-api-provider-aws cluster-api/cluster-api-provider-aws --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.3

steps.title: Create a Cluster Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster Custom Resource (CR) to define the desired state of your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: capi-system
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: AWSCluster
    name: my-cluster
EOF

```

> Creates a Cluster CR named 'my-cluster'.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: capi-system
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: AWSCluster
    name: my-cluster
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Cluster Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Cluster Custom Resource to ensure it is created and ready.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n capi-system

```

> Lists all clusters in the capi-system namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and may result in data loss. Ensure to backup any important data before proceeding.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace capi-system

helm uninstall cluster-api-provider-aws --namespace capi-system

kubectl delete namespace capi-system

```

> Uninstalls the Cluster API Helm release.
> Uninstalls the AWS provider.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API components
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure to backup your cluster state. This step upgrades the Cluster API components to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n capi-system -o yaml > backup-clusters.yaml

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.3

kubectl get clusters -n capi-system

```

> Backs up the current cluster state.
> Upgrades the Cluster API to the specified version.
> Verifies that the clusters are still present after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get clusters -n capi-system -o yaml > backup-clusters.yaml
helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.3

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD is not installed or not available in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the Cluster API components are installed correctly and the CRDs are applied.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: unable to create infrastructure'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error may occur if the infrastructure provider is not configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the provider configuration and ensure that all required credentials and permissions are set.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster is still in the process of being created or there is an issue with the infrastructure.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the cluster and the logs of the Cluster API components for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates a problem with the Kubernetes API server certificate.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your kubeconfig is pointing to the correct cluster and that the API server certificate is valid.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved error handling in the Cluster API controller.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old Cluster API v1alpha3 API version, which is no longer supported.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to migrate your Cluster CRs to the new v1beta1 API version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get clusters -n capi-system' listing your clusters with their statuses as 'Ready'.
codeSnippets: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.3
helm install cluster-api-provider-aws cluster-api/cluster-api-provider-aws --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.3

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: capi-system
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: AWSCluster
    name: my-cluster
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and have kubectl and helm installed.
